Description:en: The concept is to distinguish the stage of labor, the first stage of labor is the time of the onset of true labor until the cervix is completely dilated at 10 cm
The concept is to distinguish the stages of labor, the second stage of labor is the period after the cervix is dilated at 10 cm until the baby is delivered
The concept is to distinguish the stages of labor, the third stage of labor is the delivery of the placenta (afterbirth)
The stages of the birth process
The concept is to distinguish the stage of labor, the fourth stage of labor is the stage where the patient needs to be observed within 2 hours of post-partum
Question to answer whether companion is present during labour and delivery.
Question to answer whether pain relief drugs has been given to the client.
Question to answer whether oral fluids has been given to the client.
Lying flat on the back.
Question to record posture of the client.
A convenient set for all the supportive care components in the labour care guide.
FHR lowers below baseline usually at the start of a contraction, reaches the lowest point at the peak of the contraction and then increases at the peak of a contraction.
FHR lowers below baseline usually after the peak of a contraction.
The timing of low FHR and return to baseline in relation to the contraction is variable.

Fully specified name:en: TYPE OF FETAL HEART RATE DECELERATION
Description:en: Question to record type of fetal heart rate deceleration.
Lightly stained amniotic fluid with thin meconium, where fluid remains mostly clear and indicates mild or early fetal stress.
Increased meconium, fluid not fully clear.
Heavy meconium, opaque fluid
Is the grading of amniotic fluid that contains meconium (fetal stool) based on its appearance, thickness, and amount, used to assess possible fetal wellbeing during labour.
Refers to a condition in which the amniotic sac has not ruptured, meaning the amniotic fluid remains fully contained inside the uterus.
Is normal amniotic fluid that appears clear or slightly straw-coloured and indicates no visible fetal distress or infection at the time of observation.
The visual inspection of the amniotic fluid performed by the healthcare provider.
Status of caput succedaneum
Is a clinical assessment of the overlap of fetal skull bones (cranial sutures) during labour, used to indicate the degree of head compression as the baby passes through the birth canal.
A convenient set with questions regarding status of baby during labor.
A convenient set with questions on status of a woman during labor.
Amount of cervical dilation refers to the degree of opening of the cervix, measured in centimeters (cm) during labour.

Fully specified name:en: FREQUENCY OF UTERINE CONTRACTIONS WITHIN 10 MINUTE PERIOD
Description:en: Frequency of uterine contractions within 10 minute period.

Fully specified name:en: DESCENT OF FETAL HEAD IN RELATION TO ISCHIAL SPINES
Description:en: Proportion of head above spines out of 5.
Question to record duration of contractions in seconds.
A convenient set to with questions on labour progress of a woman during labor.
Question to answer whether oxytocin was given during this visit.
Oxytocin concentration in U/L
Oxytocin drip dose in drops/min
Intravenous fluids administration
Type of Intravenous fluids administered
A set of assessment and plan notes
